Title: Eukaryotic lipid metabolic pathway is essential for functional chloroplasts and CO2 and light responses in Arabidopsis guard cells
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small glycine-rich protein; putative regulatory component of TGD lipid transport complex
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Whole plant including guard cells; function demonstrated in guard cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chloroplast glycolipids (MGDG, DGDG; especially eukaryotic-pathway-derived 36:6-MGDG and 36:6-DGDG)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for guard cell chloroplast development and stomatal responses to CO2 and light
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GLES1 reduces ER-to-chloroplast lipid transfer, decreases eukaryotic-pathway-derived chloroplast glycolipids in guard cells, impairs thylakoid formation, and disrupts CO2- and light-responsive stomatal movement
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; mutant analysis (gles1); genomic complementation with pGLES1:GLES1-GFP; guard-cell-specific complementation with pGC1:GLES1-GFP; GFP localization; lipidomics; electron microscopy; microarray; stomatal bioassays; patch clamp
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GLES1 native promoter (constitutive/endogenous); GC1 promoter (guard cell-specific)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CO2 shifts; light illumination; ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear whole-leaf photosynthetic defect detected under tested conditions; mesophyll chloroplasts appeared normal
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLES1 was identified as At1g27695/TGD5, a chloroplast-envelope small glycine-rich protein required for guard cell chloroplast development. 2. gles1 mutants showed reduced eukaryotic-pathway-derived chloroplast glycolipids, defective thylakoid formation in guard cells, and impaired stomatal responses to CO2 and light. 3. Guard-cell-specific expression of GLES1 restored chloroplast development and stomatal function, showing a guard-cell-autonomous role.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note that besides lipid transfer, GLES1/TGD5 could also have an unknown regulatory role in CO2-induced signaling processes.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Extraplastidic TGD complex component; phosphatidic acid-binding tethering protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports chloroplast lipid supply needed for guard cell chloroplast biogenesis and CO2 response
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of TGD4 causes reduced chlorophyll fluorescence in guard cells and reduced stomatal CO2 sensitivity, supporting essential ER-to-chloroplast lipid trafficking
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis of tgd4-3; chlorophyll fluorescence assays; stomatal CO2 response assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tgd4-3 showed gles1-like achlorophyllous/reduced chlorophyll fluorescence phenotypes in guard cells. 2. tgd4-3 also showed reduced CO2 sensitivity of stomata. 3. These results support the importance of ER-to-chloroplast lipid trafficking in guard cell function.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as supportive core component tested in Results, though the main focus is GLES1/TGD5.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small ATPase / ATP-binding TGD complex component
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports guard cell chloroplast lipid import and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown tgd3-1 reduces guard cell chlorophyll fluorescence, indicating contribution to ER-to-chloroplast lipid transfer, but residual activity is sufficient to preserve stomatal CO2 responsiveness
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis of tgd3-1; chlorophyll fluorescence assays; stomatal CO2 response assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tgd3-1 exhibited reduced chlorophyll fluorescence in guard cells. 2. Unlike gles1/tgd5 and tgd4-3, tgd3-1 retained stomatal CO2 responsiveness. 3. This suggests partial lipid-transfer activity can separate chloroplast development/photosynthetic defects from CO2 signaling competence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as supportive core component tested in Results; phenotype weaker than gles1/tgd4-3.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Prokaryotic glycerolipid biosynthesis pathway in plastids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of lysophosphatidic acid from glycerol-3-phosphate as the first step of plastidial glycerolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Blocking ATS1-dependent prokaryotic pathway had no detectable effect on guard cell chlorophyll fluorescence or stomatal CO2 response, indicating the prokaryotic pathway is dispensable/strongly reduced in guard cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of ats1-1; chlorophyll fluorescence assays; stomatal CO2 response assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATS1 catalyzes the first step of the plastidial prokaryotic lipid pathway. 2. The ats1-1 mutant did not alter guard cell chlorophyll fluorescence. 3. ats1-1 also did not impair stomatal CO2 responsiveness, supporting low contribution of the prokaryotic pathway in guard cells.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as a comparative pathway mutant to infer pathway dependence in guard cells, not as the central discovery gene.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Δ7 desaturase specific to palmitate at sn-2 position of MGDG
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Palmitate esterified at the sn-2 position of MGDG
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ7 double bond into palmitate at the sn-2 position of MGDG, contributing to 34:6-MGDG formation in the prokaryotic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Comparable between guard cells and mesophyll cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Comparable between guard cells and mesophyll cells (GCP/MCP ratio 1.35 ± 0.23; P = 0.4)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Marker of prokaryotic pathway contribution to chloroplast membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FAD5 expression was not reduced in guard cells, indicating that low 34:6-MGDG in guard cells reflects decreased prokaryotic flux rather than altered FAD5 transcript abundance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray comparison of guard cells versus mesophyll cells; lipid profiling inference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD5 produces 34:6-MGDG in the prokaryotic pathway. 2. Its transcript level was similar in guard and mesophyll cells. 3. Therefore, reduced prokaryotic lipid species in guard cells are attributed to lower pathway flux, not reduced FAD5 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally manipulated in this paper; included because it is used mechanistically to interpret lipidomic results.
